## 1. Introduction and Goals

### 1.1 Requirements Overview

The **Allegro PoC** (Proof of Concept) is a modernization prototype designed to demonstrate a bridge between a modern web-based user interface and a legacy Java Swing desktop application named **ALLEGRO**. The system operates in the domain of German social insurance customer management (*Kundenverwaltung*).

The core use case is:

> A caseworker uses a modern browser-based search UI to look up a customer (*Kunde*) by name, address, or other personal details, selects a payment recipient (*Zahlungsempf├żnger*), and transfers the selected data in real time into the legacy ALLEGRO desktop application ŌĆö without any manual copy-paste.

**Key functional requirements identified from source code:**

| ID   | Requirement                                                                                      |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| FR-1 | Search for persons (Kunden) by name, first name, ZIP code, city, street, and house number        |
| FR-2 | Display a list of matching search results                                                         |
| FR-3 | Select a payment recipient (Zahlungsempf├żnger) with IBAN, BIC, and validity date                |
| FR-4 | Transfer selected person and payment data to the ALLEGRO desktop application via WebSocket        |
| FR-5 | Receive and display transferred data inside the ALLEGRO Swing client form fields                  |
| FR-6 | Support free-text textarea synchronisation between clients                                        |
| FR-7 | Submit collected form data via HTTP POST to a backend service (MVP variant)                      |

### 1.2 Quality Goals

The following top quality goals were identified from the system design and PoC context:

| Priority | Quality Goal                    | Description                                                                                          |
|----------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1        | **Real-Time Responsiveness**    | Data selected in the browser must appear instantly in the Swing client via WebSocket push            |
| 2        | **Interoperability**            | Modern web client and legacy Swing desktop client must seamlessly exchange structured JSON data      |
| 3        | **Simplicity / PoC Fitness**    | The architecture must be lightweight enough to demonstrate the concept with minimal infrastructure   |
| 4        | **Extensibility**               | The MVP pattern in the Java client supports future replacement of UI or backend without full rewrite |

### 1.3 Stakeholders

| Stakeholder                  | Role / Interest                                                                                    |
|------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Caseworker (Sachbearbeiter)  | End user of both ALLEGRO desktop and the modern search UI; benefits from faster data entry         |
| ALLEGRO Product Owner        | Interested in modernization feasibility without full rewrite of the legacy desktop application      |
| Development Team             | Implements and maintains the PoC; needs clean architecture for future extension                     |
| Architect / Reviewer         | Evaluates modernization approach; reviews PoC for production readiness assessment                   |
| IT Operations                | Responsible for deployment of Node.js server and Docker-based HTTPBin service                      |

---

## 2. Constraints

### 2.1 Technical Constraints

| Constraint                    | Description                                                                                              |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Java SDK Ōēź 22.0.1**        | The Java Swing application requires Java 22 or higher (configured in `pom.xml` compiler plugin)          |
| **WebSocket Protocol**        | Communication between clients is exclusively via WebSocket (RFC 6455) on port `1337`                     |
| **Node.js Runtime**           | The message broker server requires a Node.js runtime environment                                         |
| **Localhost-only networking** | All services are hardcoded to `localhost`; no distributed deployment is supported in the PoC             |
| **No Persistence**            | No database; the Vue.js client uses hardcoded in-memory test data                                        |
| **HTTP on port 8080**         | The HTTP backend endpoint (HTTPBin/mock) must listen on `http://localhost:8080`                           |
| **Maven Build (Java)**        | The Java component uses Apache Maven as its build system                                                  |
| **Vue CLI 4.x**               | The Vue.js client is scaffolded with Vue CLI 4 and requires Node.js + Yarn for build and dev server       |
| **Docker required**           | The HTTPBin mock service (`kennethreitz/httpbin`) runs in Docker on port 8080                            |

### 2.2 Organisational Constraints

| Constraint               | Description                                                                                          |
|--------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **PoC Scope**            | This is explicitly a Proof of Concept; production hardening (auth, persistence, scaling) is out of scope |
| **Hardcoded Test Data**  | Person/customer data is hardcoded in the Vue component; no integration with real backend              |
| **Single Developer Context** | Directory paths in doc files contain personal developer paths (e.g., `/c/Users/esultano/`)       |
| **No Test Coverage**     | No automated tests exist in any component                                                             |

### 2.3 Conventions

| Convention               | Description                                                                                |
|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **JSON messaging**       | All WebSocket messages are JSON-encoded with `target` and `content` fields                 |
| **German UI language**   | All UI labels are in German (Vorname, Name, Geburtsdatum, PLZ, Ort, Strasse, etc.)          |
| **MVP pattern**          | The Java PoC variant follows Model-View-Presenter for UI/logic separation                  |
| **OpenAPI 3.0.1**        | The REST API is documented in `api.yml` following OpenAPI specification                    |

---

## 3. Context and Scope

### 3.1 Business Context

The Allegro PoC sits at the intersection of a modern browser-based client and a legacy desktop application. The system acts as a real-time data bridge: data entered or searched in the new Vue.js UI flows immediately into the ALLEGRO legacy Swing forms, eliminating manual data re-entry.

```mermaid
graph LR
    classDef actor fill:#f5a623,stroke:#d4870a,color:#000
    classDef system fill:#1a6bb5,stroke:#0d4a8a,color:#fff
    classDef external fill:#6c757d,stroke:#495057,color:#fff

    Caseworker["­¤æż Caseworker\n(Sachbearbeiter)"]:::actor
    VueUI["­¤īÉ Vue.js Search UI\n(Modern Browser Client)"]:::system
    AllegroSwing["­¤¢ź’ĖÅ ALLEGRO Desktop\n(Java Swing Client)"]:::system
    HTTPBin["­¤É│ HTTPBin Service\n(Mock HTTP Backend)"]:::external
    CustomerData["­¤ōŗ Customer Data\n(Hardcoded / Future DB)"]:::external

    Caseworker -->|"searches persons"| VueUI
    Caseworker -->|"views & fills forms"| AllegroSwing
    VueUI -->|"transfers selected data\n(via WebSocket)"| AllegroSwing
    AllegroSwing -->|"HTTP POST form data"| HTTPBin
    VueUI -.->|"reads"| CustomerData
```

**External interfaces:**

| Interface              | Direction     | Protocol        | Description                                           |
|------------------------|---------------|-----------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| Caseworker ŌåÆ Vue UI    | Inbound       | HTTP/Browser    | User searches and selects customer data               |
| Vue UI ŌåÆ ALLEGRO       | Internal      | WebSocket/JSON  | Data transfer via Node.js broker                      |
| ALLEGRO ŌåÆ HTTPBin      | Outbound      | HTTP REST/JSON  | Form data submission to mock backend service          |
| Caseworker ŌåÆ ALLEGRO   | Inbound       | Desktop UI      | Direct form interaction in Swing application          |

### 3.2 Technical Context

```mermaid
graph TB
    classDef browser fill:#28a745,stroke:#1a7a30,color:#fff
    classDef java fill:#dc3545,stroke:#a71d2a,color:#fff
    classDef nodejs fill:#68b82e,stroke:#4a8020,color:#fff
    classDef docker fill:#0db7ed,stroke:#0891c4,color:#fff

    subgraph "Browser Environment"
        VueSPA["Vue.js 2 SPA\n(node-vue-client)\nServed by vue-cli-service"]:::browser
    end

    subgraph "Node.js Process"
        WSServer["Node.js WebSocket Server\n(WebsocketServer.js)\nPort: 1337"]:::nodejs
    end

    subgraph "JVM Process (Java 22)"
        SwingWS["Java Swing WS Client\n(websocket.Main)\nTyrus Client"]:::java
        SwingMVP["Java Swing MVP Client\n(com.Main)\nPresenter + Model"]:::java
    end

    subgraph "Docker Container"
        HTTPBin["HTTPBin\n(kennethreitz/httpbin)\nPort: 8080"]:::docker
    end

    VueSPA -->|"ws://localhost:1337\nWebSocket (RFC 6455)"| WSServer
    SwingWS -->|"ws://localhost:1337\nWebSocket (Tyrus)"| WSServer
    WSServer -->|"broadcast JSON to all"| VueSPA
    WSServer -->|"broadcast JSON to all"| SwingWS
    SwingMVP -->|"HTTP POST\nhttp://localhost:8080/post\napplication/json"| HTTPBin
```

**Technical interfaces:**

| Interface                    | Technology             | Port  | Message Format                          |
|------------------------------|------------------------|-------|-----------------------------------------|
| Vue.js Ōåö WS Server          | WebSocket (native API) | 1337  | JSON: `{ target, content }`             |
| Swing WS Client Ōåö WS Server | WebSocket (Tyrus 1.15) | 1337  | JSON: `{ target, content }`             |
| Swing MVP ŌåÆ HTTPBin          | HTTP POST (JDK)        | 8080  | JSON: flat key/value (ModelProperties)  |
| WS Server broadcast          | Node.js `websocket`    | 1337  | Broadcast to all connected clients      |

---

## 4. Solution Strategy

### 4.1 Fundamental Technology Decisions

| Decision                          | Technology Chosen                            | Rationale                                                                               |
|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Real-time data bridge**         | WebSocket (RFC 6455)                         | Enables push-based, bidirectional communication; avoids polling; supported natively in browsers and via Tyrus in Java |
| **Message broker / hub**          | Node.js with `websocket` npm package         | Lightweight, event-driven; simple broadcast server requires minimal code                |
| **Legacy client UI**              | Java Swing                                   | Existing ALLEGRO system uses Swing; PoC retains this to demonstrate non-invasive integration |
| **Modern search UI**              | Vue.js 2.x SPA                               | Lightweight, component-based; Vue CLI enables rapid prototyping                          |
| **Java WebSocket client library** | Tyrus Standalone Client 1.15                 | Reference implementation of JSR-356 (javax.websocket); self-contained, no app server needed |
| **Java JSON processing**          | javax.json-api + Glassfish javax.json        | Standard Java EE JSON processing API; streaming parser for low-overhead JSON parsing    |
| **HTTP mock backend**             | HTTPBin (Docker)                             | Zero-effort HTTP echo service for validating HTTP POST from Swing MVP client            |
| **UI architecture (Java)**        | MVP (Model-View-Presenter)                   | Separates UI (Swing widgets) from business logic; improves testability and maintainability |
| **Build system (Java)**           | Maven                                        | Standard Java build tool; manages Tyrus and javax.json dependencies                     |

### 4.2 Top-Level Decomposition Strategy

The system follows a **Hub-and-Spoke** topology for WebSocket messaging:

```mermaid
graph TB
    classDef hub fill:#dc3545,stroke:#a71d2a,color:#fff
    classDef spoke fill:#1a6bb5,stroke:#0d4a8a,color:#fff
    classDef future fill:#6c757d,stroke:#495057,color:#fff,stroke-dasharray: 5 5

    Hub["Node.js WS Server\n(Central Hub)\nBroadcasts to ALL clients"]:::hub
    Vue["Vue.js Client\n(Spoke #1)\nSends: search results + textarea"]:::spoke
    Swing["Swing WS Client\n(Spoke #2)\nReceives: textfield + textarea"]:::spoke
    Future["Future Clients\n(Spoke #N)\nExtensible"]:::future

    Vue -->|"sendUTF (JSON)"| Hub
    Hub -->|"broadcast"| Vue
    Hub -->|"broadcast"| Swing
    Hub -.->|"broadcast"| Future
    Swing -->|"sendUTF (JSON)"| Hub
```

### 4.3 Approach to Quality Goals

| Quality Goal                | Architectural Approach                                                                              |
|-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Real-Time Responsiveness    | WebSocket push eliminates polling latency; server broadcasts synchronously to all clients           |
| Interoperability            | All messages are JSON-serialized with a `target` + `content` envelope; clients dispatch by target  |
| Simplicity                  | No framework overhead on server side; single-file Node.js server; Vue CLI scaffolding for client    |
| Extensibility               | MVP pattern decouples Swing UI from model; new clients can connect to WS server without server changes |

---

## 5. Building Block View

### 5.1 Level 1: System Overview

```mermaid
graph TB
    classDef client fill:#1a6bb5,stroke:#0d4a8a,color:#fff
    classDef server fill:#28a745,stroke:#1a7a30,color:#fff
    classDef external fill:#6c757d,stroke:#495057,color:#fff

    subgraph AllegroPoC["Allegro PoC System"]
        VueClient["node-vue-client\nVue.js Browser Application\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó Person search\nŌĆó Payment recipient selection\nŌĆó Data export to ALLEGRO"]:::client
        WSServer["node-server\nNode.js WebSocket Server\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó Client registry\nŌĆó Message broadcast\nŌĆó HTTP + WS hybrid server"]:::server
        SwingClient["swing\nJava Swing Desktop Application\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó ALLEGRO form display\nŌĆó WebSocket data receiver\nŌĆó HTTP form submission (MVP variant)"]:::client
    end

    HTTPBin["HTTPBin\n(External Docker Service)\nPort 8080"]:::external

    VueClient <-->|"ws://localhost:1337"| WSServer
    SwingClient <-->|"ws://localhost:1337"| WSServer
    SwingClient -->|"HTTP POST :8080"| HTTPBin
```

### 5.2 Level 2: Module Structure

```mermaid
graph TB
    classDef entry fill:#28a745,stroke:#1a7a30,color:#fff
    classDef component fill:#1a6bb5,stroke:#0d4a8a,color:#fff
    classDef config fill:#6c757d,stroke:#495057,color:#fff

    main["main.js\nApplication Entry Point\nMounts Vue instance to #app"]:::entry
    App["App.vue\nRoot Component\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó Red Allegro header bar\nŌĆó Logo area\nŌĆó Hosts Search component"]:::component
    Search["Search.vue\nMain Business Component\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó Person search form\nŌĆó Search results table\nŌĆó Zahlungsempf├żnger table\nŌĆó WebSocket client (ws://localhost:1337)\nŌĆó 'Nach ALLEGRO ├╝bernehmen' action"]:::component
    indexHtml["public/index.html\nHTML Shell\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó SPA mount point (#app)"]:::config
    pkg["package.json\nDependencies\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó vue ^2.6.10\nŌĆó core-js ^3.1.2"]:::config

    main --> App
    App --> Search
    indexHtml --> main
```

#### 5.2.2 node-server (Node.js WebSocket Server)

```mermaid
graph TB
    classDef server fill:#68b82e,stroke:#4a8020,color:#fff
    classDef state fill:#fd7e14,stroke:#e36209,color:#000
    classDef event fill:#6f42c1,stroke:#4e2d8c,color:#fff

    WSServerMain["WebsocketServer.js\nMain Server Module\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó HTTP server creation (port 1337)\nŌĆó WebSocket server setup\nŌĆó Dependency: 'websocket' npm package"]:::server
    ClientState["Client Registry\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó clients[] array\nŌĆó Connection index tracking\nŌĆó Splice on disconnect"]:::state
    MessageState["Message Log\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó messages[] array\n(declared but unused)"]:::state
    Events["Event Handlers\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó on('request'): accept connection\nŌĆó on('message'): broadcast UTF8\nŌĆó on('close'): remove from registry"]:::event

    WSServerMain --> ClientState
    WSServerMain --> MessageState
    WSServerMain --> Events
    Events --> ClientState
```

#### 5.2.3 swing (Java Application ŌĆö Two Parallel Variants)

```mermaid
graph TB
    classDef variantHeader fill:#343a40,stroke:#1a1e21,color:#fff
    classDef class fill:#17a2b8,stroke:#0d7a8a,color:#fff
    classDef inner fill:#6f42c1,stroke:#4e2d8c,color:#fff

    subgraph WSVariant["WebSocket Variant ŌĆö package: websocket"]
        WSMain["websocket.Main\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó Swing UI setup (GridBagLayout)\nŌĆó WS client integration\nŌĆó JSON streaming parser\nŌĆó Message dispatch (target switch)"]:::class
        WSEndpoint["WebsocketClientEndpoint\n(inner class)\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó @OnOpen / @OnClose / @OnMessage\nŌĆó sendMessage() via AsyncRemote"]:::inner
        WSMessage["Message\n(inner class)\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó target: String\nŌĆó content: String"]:::inner
        WSSearchResult["SearchResult\n(inner class)\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó name, first, dob\nŌĆó zip, ort, street, hausnr\nŌĆó ze_iban, ze_bic, ze_valid_from"]:::inner
        WSMain --> WSEndpoint
        WSMain --> WSMessage
        WSMain --> WSSearchResult
    end

    subgraph MVPVariant["MVP Variant ŌĆö package: com.poc"]
        ComMain["com.Main\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó Wires MVP triad\nŌĆó CountDownLatch lifecycle"]:::class
        PocView["PocView\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó JFrame, JTextArea\nŌĆó JTextField ├Ś 9\nŌĆó JRadioButton ├Ś 3 (gender)\nŌĆó JButton (Anordnen)"]:::class
        PocPresenter["PocPresenter\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó DocumentListener bindings\nŌĆó Button action handler\nŌĆó EventEmitter subscriber"]:::class
        PocModel["PocModel\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó EnumMap model state\nŌĆó action() ŌåÆ HTTP POST"]:::class
        HttpBinSvc["HttpBinService\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó POST http://localhost:8080/post\nŌĆó javax.json generator\nŌĆó Scanner response reader"]:::class
        EventEmitter["EventEmitter\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó subscribe(listener)\nŌĆó emit(eventData)"]:::class
        ValueModel["ValueModel T\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó Generic field wrapper\nŌĆó getField() / setField()"]:::class
        ModelProps["ModelProperties (enum)\nŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\nŌĆó TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME,\n  LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH,\n  ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN,\n  BIC, VALID_FROM,\n  FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE"]:::class

        ComMain --> PocView
        ComMain --> PocModel
        ComMain --> PocPresenter
        ComMain --> EventEmitter
        PocPresenter --> PocView
        PocPresenter --> PocModel
        PocPresenter --> EventEmitter
        PocModel --> HttpBinSvc
        PocModel --> EventEmitter
        PocModel --> ValueModel
        PocModel --> ModelProps
    end
```

### 5.3 Level 3: Java MVP Class Diagram

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Main {
        +main(String[] args) void
    }
    class PocView {
        +JFrame frame
        +JTextArea textArea
        +JTextField name
        +JTextField firstName
        +JTextField dateOfBirth
        +JTextField zip
        +JTextField ort
        +JTextField street
        +JTextField iban
        +JTextField bic
        +JTextField validFrom
        +JRadioButton female
        +JRadioButton male
        +JRadioButton diverse
        +ButtonGroup gender
        +JButton button
        -initUI() void
    }
    class PocPresenter {
        -PocView view
        -PocModel model
        +PocPresenter(PocView, PocModel, EventEmitter)
        -bind(JTextComponent, ModelProperties) void
        -bind(JRadioButton, ModelProperties) void
        -initializeBindings() void
    }
    class PocModel {
        +Map~ModelProperties_ValueModel~ model
        -HttpBinService httpBinService
        -EventEmitter eventEmitter
        +PocModel(EventEmitter)
        +action() void
    }
    class HttpBinService {
        +String URL
        +String PATH
        +String CONTENT_TYPE
        +post(Map) String
    }
    class EventEmitter {
        -List~EventListener~ listeners
        +subscribe(EventListener) void
        +emit(String eventData) void
    }
    class EventListener {
        <<interface>>
        +onEvent(String eventData) void
    }
    class ValueModel {
        -T field
        +getField() T
        +setField(T field) void
    }
    class ModelProperties {
        <<enumeration>>
        TEXT_AREA
        FIRST_NAME
        LAST_NAME
        DATE_OF_BIRTH
        ZIP
        ORT
        STREET
        IBAN
        BIC
        VALID_FROM
        FEMALE
        MALE
        DIVERSE
    }

    Main --> PocView
    Main --> PocModel
    Main --> PocPresenter
    Main --> EventEmitter
    PocPresenter --> PocView
    PocPresenter --> PocModel
    PocPresenter --> EventEmitter
    PocModel --> HttpBinService
    PocModel --> EventEmitter
    PocModel "1" --> "*" ValueModel
    PocModel --> ModelProperties
    EventEmitter "1" --> "*" EventListener
```

### 5.4 Level 3: Java WebSocket Variant Class Diagram

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Main {
        -CountDownLatch latch$
        -JFrame frame$
        -JTextArea textArea$
        -JTextField tf_name$
        -JTextField tf_first$
        -JTextField tf_dob$
        -JTextField tf_zip$
        -JTextField tf_ort$
        -JTextField tf_street$
        -JTextField tf_hausnr$
        -JTextField tf_ze_iban$
        -JTextField tf_ze_bic$
        -JTextField tf_ze_valid_from$
        -JsonParserFactory jsonParserFactory$
        +main(String[]) void$
        -initUI() void$
        +toSearchResult(String) SearchResult$
        +extract(String) Message$
    }
    class WebsocketClientEndpoint {
        +Session userSession
        +WebsocketClientEndpoint(URI endpointURI)
        +onOpen(Session) void
        +onClose(Session, CloseReason) void
        +onMessage(String json) void
        +sendMessage(String message) void
    }
    class Message {
        +String target
        +String content
        +Message(String target, String message)
    }
    class SearchResult {
        +String name
        +String first
        +String dob
        +String zip
        +String ort
        +String street
        +String hausnr
        +String ze_iban
        +String ze_bic
        +String ze_valid_from
    }

    Main +-- WebsocketClientEndpoint : inner class
    Main +-- Message : inner class
    Main +-- SearchResult : inner class
    Main --> WebsocketClientEndpoint : creates
```

---

## 6. Runtime View

### 6.1 Scenario: Person Search and Transfer to ALLEGRO

This is the primary use case of the system ŌĆö a caseworker finds a customer in the Vue.js UI and sends the data to the ALLEGRO desktop application.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor CW as Caseworker
    participant Vue as Vue.js Search UI
    participant WS as Node.js WS Server
    participant Swing as ALLEGRO Swing Client

    Note over Vue, WS: Initial Connection Phase
    Vue->>WS: HTTP Upgrade ŌåÆ WebSocket (ws://localhost:1337)
    WS-->>Vue: 101 Switching Protocols (connection accepted)
    Swing->>WS: HTTP Upgrade ŌåÆ WebSocket (ws://localhost:1337)
    WS-->>Swing: 101 Switching Protocols (connection accepted)
    Note over WS: clients[] = [Vue, Swing]

    Note over CW, Vue: Search Phase
    CW->>Vue: Enter search criteria (Name / Vorname / PLZ / Ort / Strasse)
    CW->>Vue: Click "Suchen" button
    Vue->>Vue: searchPerson() ŌĆö filter search_space[] in-memory
    Vue-->>CW: Display matching results in table

    Note over CW, Vue: Selection Phase
    CW->>Vue: Click result row ŌåÆ selectResult(item)
    Vue->>Vue: selected_result = item
    CW->>Vue: Click Zahlungsempf├żnger row
    Vue->>Vue: zahlungsempfaengerSelected(item)

    Note over CW, Swing: Data Transfer Phase
    CW->>Vue: Click "Nach ALLEGRO ├╝bernehmen"
    Vue->>Vue: sendMessage(selected_result, 'textfield')
    Vue->>WS: WebSocket.send(JSON.stringify({target:"textfield", content:{...person, zahlungsempfaenger:{...}}}))
    WS->>WS: message.type === 'utf8' ŌåÆ iterate clients[]
    WS->>Vue: sendUTF(json) ŌĆö broadcast back to sender too
    WS->>Swing: sendUTF(json) ŌĆö broadcast to Swing client
    Swing->>Swing: onMessage(json) ŌåÆ extract() ŌåÆ switch("textfield")
    Swing->>Swing: toSearchResult(json) ŌåÆ streaming JSON parse
    Swing->>Swing: tf_name.setText() / tf_first.setText() / tf_dob.setText() / ...
    Swing-->>CW: All form fields populated with customer data
```

### 6.2 Scenario: Textarea Real-Time Synchronisation

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor CW as Caseworker
    participant Vue as Vue.js Search UI
    participant WS as Node.js WS Server
    participant Swing as ALLEGRO Swing Client

    CW->>Vue: Type text in textarea (v-model: internal_content_textarea)
    Vue->>Vue: watch.internal_content_textarea fires
    Vue->>Vue: sendMessage(val, "textarea")
    Vue->>WS: WebSocket.send(JSON.stringify({target:"textarea", content:"typed text..."}))
    WS->>WS: Broadcast to all clients[]
    WS->>Swing: sendUTF(json)
    Swing->>Swing: onMessage(json) ŌåÆ extract() ŌåÆ switch("textarea")
    Swing->>Swing: textArea.setText(message.content)
    Swing-->>CW: Textarea in ALLEGRO updated in real time
```

### 6.3 Scenario: HTTP Form Submission (MVP Variant ŌĆö com.poc)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor CW as Caseworker
    participant View as PocView (Swing)
    participant Presenter as PocPresenter
    participant Model as PocModel
    participant SVC as HttpBinService
    participant HTTPBin as HTTPBin Docker :8080
    participant EE as EventEmitter

    CW->>View: Fill in form fields (Vorname, Name, IBAN, BIC ...)
    View->>Presenter: DocumentListener ŌåÆ insertUpdate/removeUpdate
    Presenter->>Model: model.get(prop).setField(content)
    Note over Model: ValueModel updated per ModelProperty key

    CW->>View: Click "Anordnen" button
    View->>Presenter: ActionListener fires
    Presenter->>Model: model.action()
    Model->>Model: Build HashMap from all ModelProperties values
    Model->>SVC: httpBinService.post(data)
    SVC->>HTTPBin: HTTP POST /post\nContent-Type: application/json\n{FIRST_NAME:..., IBAN:..., ...}
    HTTPBin-->>SVC: 200 OK ŌĆö JSON echo of request
    SVC-->>Model: responseBody (String)
    Model->>EE: eventEmitter.emit(responseBody)
    EE->>Presenter: listener.onEvent(eventData) [lambda]
    Presenter->>View: view.textArea.setText(eventData)
    Presenter->>View: Clear all input fields (setText(""))
    Presenter->>View: view.female.setSelected(true)
    View-->>CW: Response shown in textarea; form cleared
```

### 6.4 WebSocket Server Connection Lifecycle (State View)

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Listening : server.listen(1337)
    Listening --> RequestReceived : wsServer.on('request')
    RequestReceived --> Connected : request.accept(null, origin)
    note right of Connected
        clients.push(connection)
        index = clients.length - 1
    end note
    Connected --> MessageReceived : connection.on('message')
    MessageReceived --> Broadcasting : message.type === 'utf8'
    Broadcasting --> Connected : for each client: sendUTF(json)
    Connected --> Disconnected : connection.on('close')
    Disconnected --> Listening : clients.splice(index, 1)
    Disconnected --> [*]
```

---

## 7. Deployment View

### 7.1 Local Development Deployment Topology

All components are designed to run on a single developer workstation. This is the only supported deployment topology for the PoC.

```mermaid
graph TB
    classDef machine fill:#343a40,stroke:#1a1e21,color:#fff
    classDef process fill:#1a6bb5,stroke:#0d4a8a,color:#fff
    classDef docker fill:#0db7ed,stroke:#0891c4,color:#fff
    classDef browser fill:#28a745,stroke:#1a7a30,color:#fff

    subgraph DevMachine["­¤¢ź’ĖÅ Developer Workstation (localhost)"]
        subgraph BrowserProc["Browser Process"]
            VueSPA["Vue.js SPA\n(node-vue-client)\nServed: vue-cli-service serve\nDev port: 8081"]:::browser
        end

        subgraph NodeProc["Node.js Process"]
            WSServer["WebsocketServer.js\nHTTP + WebSocket Server\nPort: 1337"]:::process
        end

        subgraph JVMProc["JVM Process ŌĆö Java 22"]
            SwingApp["Java Swing Application\ncom.Main (MVP)\nor websocket.Main (WS)\nDesktop GUI Window"]:::process
        end

        subgraph DockerEngine["­¤É│ Docker Engine"]
            HTTPBinCont["kennethreitz/httpbin\nHTTP Echo Service\n8080 (host) ŌåÆ 80 (container)"]:::docker
        end

        WSServer <-->|"ws://localhost:1337\nWebSocket broadcast"| VueSPA
        WSServer <-->|"ws://localhost:1337\nWebSocket broadcast"| SwingApp
        SwingApp -->|"HTTP POST\nhttp://localhost:8080/post"| HTTPBinCont
    end
```

### 7.2 Infrastructure Requirements

| Component                | Runtime           | Version   | Port       | Start Command                                          |
|--------------------------|-------------------|-----------|------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| Node.js WS Server        | Node.js           | Any LTS   | **1337**   | `node node-server/src/WebsocketServer.js`              |
| Vue.js Dev Server        | Node.js + Vue CLI | CLI 4.x   | **8081**   | `cd node-vue-client && yarn run serve`                 |
| Java Swing App (MVP)     | JVM 22+           | Ōēź 22.0.1  | ŌĆö          | Run `com.Main` via IntelliJ or `mvn exec:java`         |
| Java Swing App (WS)      | JVM 22+           | Ōēź 22.0.1  | ŌĆö          | Run `websocket.Main` via IntelliJ                      |
| HTTPBin (Docker)         | Docker            | Any       | **8080**   | `docker run -p 8080:80 kennethreitz/httpbin`           |

### 7.3 Required Startup Sequence

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    classDef step fill:#1a6bb5,stroke:#0d4a8a,color:#fff
    classDef ready fill:#28a745,stroke:#1a7a30,color:#fff

    S1["1’ĖÅŌāŻ Start Docker\n& HTTPBin container\nPort: 8080"]:::step
    S2["2’ĖÅŌāŻ Start Node.js\nWebSocket Server\nPort: 1337"]:::step
    S3["3’ĖÅŌāŻ Launch Java\nSwing Application\n(connects to :1337)"]:::step
    S4["4’ĖÅŌāŻ Start Vue.js\nDev Server & open browser\n(connects to :1337)"]:::step
    Ready["Ō£ģ System Ready\nAll clients connected\nto WebSocket hub"]:::ready

    S1 --> S2 --> S3 --> S4 --> Ready
```

### 7.4 Maven Build Configuration

```mermaid
graph LR
    classDef dep fill:#fd7e14,stroke:#e36209,color:#000
    classDef build fill:#6f42c1,stroke:#4e2d8c,color:#fff

    Maven["pom.xml\ngroupId: websocket_swing\nartifactId: websocket_swing\nversion: 0.0.1-SNAPSHOT\nJava source/target: 22"]:::build
    D1["glassfish websocket-api\n0.2"]:::dep
    D2["tyrus-standalone-client\n1.15"]:::dep

    D5["javax.json-api\n1.1.4"]:::dep
    D6["javax.json (glassfish)\n1.0.4"]:::dep

    Maven --> D1
    Maven --> D2
    Maven --> D3
    Maven --> D4
    Maven --> D5
    Maven --> D6
```

## 8. Crosscutting Concepts

### 8.1 Domain Model

The core domain revolves around German social insurance customer management (*Kundenverwaltung*). The entities identified from source code analysis:

```mermaid
erDiagram
    KUNDE {
        string knr PK "Kundennummer (Customer No.)"
        string first "Vorname (First Name)"
        string name "Nachname (Last Name)"
        string dob "Geburtsdatum (Date of Birth)"
        string zip "PLZ (Postal Code)"
        string ort "Ort (City)"
        string street "Strasse (Street)"
        string hausnr "Hausnummer (House Number)"
        string nationality "Nationalit├żt (optional)"
        string betriebsbez "Betriebsbezeichnung (optional)"
        string rvnr "RV-Nummer (optional)"
        string bgnr "BG-Nummer (optional)"
        string vorsatzwort "Vorsatzwort (optional)"
        string leistung "Leistung (optional)"
        string traegernr "Tr├żger-Nr. der gE. (optional)"
    }

    ZAHLUNGSEMPFAENGER {
        string iban PK "IBAN"
        string bic "BIC"
        string valid_from "G├╝ltig ab (Valid From)"
        string valid_until "G├╝ltig bis (optional)"
        string type "Typ (optional)"
    }

    WS_MESSAGE {
        string target "textarea OR textfield"
        string content "JSON payload or plain text"
    }

    GESCHLECHT {
        string value "WEIBLICH / MAENNLICH / DIVERS"
    }

    KUNDE ||--o{ ZAHLUNGSEMPFAENGER : "hat"
    KUNDE ||--|| GESCHLECHT : "hat"
    WS_MESSAGE ||--o| KUNDE : "tr├żgt"
```

### 8.2 WebSocket Messaging Architecture

**Message Envelope Format:**  
All messages exchanged via WebSocket conform to a simple JSON envelope:

```json
{
  "target": "textfield | textarea",
  "content": "<string or JSON-serialized object>"
}
```

| Target      | Content Type             | Description                                      |
|-------------|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `textfield` | JSON Object (serialized) | Serialized `Kunde` + `Zahlungsempf├żnger` data    |
| `textarea`  | Plain String             | Free-text content for textarea synchronisation   |

**Broadcast Topology:**  
The Node.js server does **not** implement selective routing. Every message received from any client is broadcast to **all connected clients** including the sender. Clients are responsible for handling their own echoed messages.

```mermaid
graph LR
    classDef client fill:#1a6bb5,stroke:#0d4a8a,color:#fff
    classDef hub fill:#dc3545,stroke:#a71d2a,color:#fff

    VueC["Vue Client"]:::client
    SwingC["Swing Client"]:::client
    Hub["WS Server\nBroadcast Hub"]:::hub

    VueC -->|"send message"| Hub
    Hub -->|"echo back"| VueC
    Hub -->|"forward"| SwingC
    SwingC -->|"send message"| Hub
    Hub -->|"forward"| VueC
    Hub -->|"echo back"| SwingC
```

### 8.3 Design Patterns Applied

```mermaid
mindmap
    root((Design Patterns\nin Allegro PoC))
        Observer Pattern
            EventEmitter class
            EventListener interface
            DocumentListener in Presenter
            WebSocket onMessage callbacks
            Vue.js watch watcher
        MVP Pattern
            PocView owns Swing widgets
            PocPresenter coordinates flow
            PocModel holds state and services
        Hub and Spoke
            Node.js as central broker
            All clients connect to hub
            Broadcast to all spokes
        Generic Wrapper
            ValueModel T generic
            Wraps any field type
            Used for all ModelProperties
        Enum-driven Map
            ModelProperties enum as key
            EnumMap for O1 access
            Drives view bindings in Presenter
        Observer via CountDownLatch
            Keeps main thread alive
            Decremented on WS close
```

### 8.4 Error Handling Assessment

| Component              | Error Handling Approach                                                                  |
|------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Node.js WS Server      | **None** ŌĆö no `connection.on('error')` handler; unhandled exceptions crash the process  |
| Vue.js Client          | **None** ŌĆö `socket.onerror` not implemented; no reconnection logic                      |
| Swing WS Client        | `DeploymentException`, `IOException`, `InterruptedException` wrapped in `RuntimeException`; no recovery |
| Swing MVP Client       | `IOException` and `InterruptedException` wrapped in `RuntimeException`; no fallback     |
| HttpBinService         | **No HTTP status validation** ŌĆö assumes 200 OK; any error throws unchecked exception    |

### 8.5 JSON Processing Strategy

Two different JSON processing approaches are used across the system:

| Component             | Library                    | Strategy               | Notes                                              |
|-----------------------|----------------------------|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| Vue.js Client         | Native browser JSON        | `JSON.stringify()`     | Simple object serialization                        |
| Swing WS Client       | javax.json Streaming API   | Event-driven parser    | Manual boolean-flag field extraction (verbose)     |
| Swing MVP Client      | javax.json Generator API   | Streaming generator    | Writes JSON to HTTP connection output stream       |
| Node.js Server        | None (raw passthrough)     | UTF-8 string relay     | No parsing/deserialization; pure message broker    |

### 8.6 UI Field Mapping

Both GUI clients share the same form field layout representing the same domain entities:

| Domain Field    | Vue.js `v-model`              | Swing (WS variant)    | Swing (MVP variant)  | ModelProperty    |
|-----------------|-------------------------------|-----------------------|----------------------|------------------|
| Vorname         | `formdata.first`              | `tf_first`            | `firstName`          | `FIRST_NAME`     |
| Name            | `formdata.last`               | `tf_name`             | `name`               | `LAST_NAME`      |
| Geburtsdatum    | `formdata.dob`                | `tf_dob`              | `dateOfBirth`        | `DATE_OF_BIRTH`  |
| Strasse         | `formdata.street`             | `tf_street`           | `street`             | `STREET`         |
| PLZ             | `formdata.zip`                | `tf_zip`              | `zip`                | `ZIP`            |
| Ort             | `formdata.ort`                | `tf_ort`              | `ort`                | `ORT`            |
| IBAN            | `zahlungsempfaenger.iban`     | `tf_ze_iban`          | `iban`               | `IBAN`           |
| BIC             | `zahlungsempfaenger.bic`      | `tf_ze_bic`           | `bic`                | `BIC`            |
| G├╝ltig ab       | `zahlungsempfaenger.valid_from` | `tf_ze_valid_from`  | `validFrom`          | `VALID_FROM`     |
| Geschlecht      | *(not in Vue search form)*    | `rb_female/male/diverse` | `female/male/diverse` | `FEMALE/MALE/DIVERSE` |
| Textbereich     | `internal_content_textarea`   | `textArea`            | `textArea`           | `TEXT_AREA`      |

---

## 9. Architecture Decisions

### ADR-001: WebSocket as the Primary Communication Protocol

| | |
|---|---|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | PoC initial design |
| **Context** | The ALLEGRO legacy system is a Java Swing desktop application. The new interface is a browser-based SPA. Bridging these two in real time across language and runtime boundaries requires a communication mechanism that works for both without heavyweight middleware. |
| **Decision** | Use WebSocket (RFC 6455) as the transport protocol. The browser's native `WebSocket` API and the Java Tyrus standalone client both support the same protocol without requiring an application server. |
| **Consequences** | Ō£ģ No polling needed; push-based real-time communication. Ō£ģ Bi-directional channel supports future SwingŌåÆBrowser flows. Ō£ģ Both browser and Java have first-class WebSocket support. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ All clients receive all messages (broadcast). ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ No message persistence ŌĆö offline clients miss messages. |

---

### ADR-002: Node.js as Central Message Broker

| | |
|---|---|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | PoC initial design |
| **Context** | A central relay is needed between the Vue.js browser client and the Java Swing desktop client. Browser clients cannot initiate connections to desktop apps directly; a server-side hub is required. |
| **Decision** | Use a lightweight Node.js server with the `websocket` npm package as the hub. It accepts WebSocket connections and broadcasts every received message to all connected clients. |
| **Consequences** | Ō£ģ Single-file implementation; minimal complexity. Ō£ģ Node.js event loop naturally handles concurrent connections. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Broadcast-to-all topology is not suitable for multi-user production. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ No CORS restriction ŌĆö any origin is accepted (`request.accept(null, request.origin)`). |

---

### ADR-003: MVP Pattern for the Java Client

| | |
|---|---|
| **Status** | Accepted (in `com.poc` variant) |
| **Date** | PoC second iteration |
| **Context** | The `websocket.Main` variant monolithically combines UI setup, WebSocket handling, and JSON parsing in a single class with inner types. This is difficult to test and extend. |
| **Decision** | Refactor into Model-View-Presenter: `PocView` owns all Swing widgets; `PocModel` holds form state and HTTP service calls; `PocPresenter` wires them with `DocumentListener` bindings and event subscriptions. |
| **Consequences** | Ō£ģ Clear separation of concerns across three classes. Ō£ģ `PocModel` can be unit-tested without Swing. Ō£ģ View is replaceable without changing business logic. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ `PocPresenter` still has direct references to Swing-specific `JTextComponent` and `JRadioButton`. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Duplicates the UI layout defined in the WS variant. |

---

### ADR-004: JSON Message Envelope with `target` Dispatch Field

| | |
|---|---|
| **Status** | Accepted |
| **Date** | PoC initial design |
| **Context** | Two types of payload need to flow through the same WebSocket channel: structured person/payment data (for form fields) and free text (for textarea sync). |
| **Decision** | Wrap all messages in `{ "target": "<destination>", "content": <payload> }`. Receiving clients dispatch to the correct UI element based on the `target` string value. |
| **Consequences** | Ō£ģ Extensible ŌĆö new target types can be added without protocol changes. Ō£ģ Consistent across both Java and JavaScript clients. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Java implements a verbose flag-based streaming parser for extraction. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ No message schema validation ŌĆö malformed JSON causes runtime exceptions. |

---

### ADR-005: In-Memory Hardcoded Person Data in Vue.js

| | |
|---|---|
| **Status** | Accepted (PoC scope only) |
| **Date** | PoC initial design |
| **Context** | The PoC needs realistic German customer data to demonstrate search and transfer flow without a live backend or database. |
| **Decision** | Embed a hardcoded `search_space` array of 5 fictional customers with addresses and IBAN/BIC data directly in `Search.vue`. |
| **Consequences** | Ō£ģ Zero infrastructure needed for search/display features. Ō£ģ Immediately runnable without database setup. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Not representative of production scale or query complexity. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Data changes require code modification and Vue rebuild. |

---

### ADR-006: Two Parallel Java Implementation Variants

| | |
|---|---|
| **Status** | Accepted (for experimentation) |
| **Date** | PoC second iteration |
| **Context** | Two distinct integration patterns exist: WebSocket-based push reception (`websocket.Main`) and HTTP-based form submission (`com.Main`). |
| **Decision** | Maintain both as parallel exploratory implementations within the same Maven project. |
| **Consequences** | Ō£ģ Each variant isolates a specific architectural concern. Ō£ģ Teams can independently evaluate WebSocket vs. HTTP approaches. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Significant UI code duplication (GridBagLayout setup is nearly identical). ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ No clear production path ŌĆö both are PoC-quality prototypes. |

---

## 10. Quality Requirements

### 10.1 Quality Tree

```mermaid
mindmap
    root((Quality Goals))
        Performance
            Real-time latency
                WebSocket push under 100ms local
                No polling overhead
            Throughput
                Broadcast to N clients
                Single-threaded Node event loop
        Reliability
            Connection resilience
                No reconnect in Vue client
                No reconnect in Swing client
                CountDownLatch for app lifecycle
            Error recovery
                No error handlers on WS close/error
                RuntimeException on all failures
        Maintainability
            Code structure
                MVP pattern in com.poc variant
                Monolithic class in websocket variant
                Single-file Node.js server
            Test coverage
                Zero automated tests across all components
        Security
            Authentication
                None ŌĆö open WebSocket server
                No origin validation or token check
            Data protection
                PII sent unencrypted over WebSocket
                No TLS / no WSS
        Usability
            Language consistency
                All labels in German
                Field names match between clients
            Data completeness
                All domain fields represented in forms
```

### 10.2 Quality Scenarios

| ID    | Quality Attribute     | Scenario                                                                    | Expected Response                                  | Current Status          |
|-------|-----------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| QS-01 | Performance           | Caseworker clicks "Nach ALLEGRO ├╝bernehmen"; Swing updates                  | < 200 ms on localhost                               | Ō£ģ Met (local WS)        |
| QS-02 | Reliability           | Node.js WS server restarts; clients recover automatically                   | Auto-reconnect with backoff                         | ŌØī Not implemented       |
| QS-03 | Security              | Unknown client connects to ws://localhost:1337 and injects messages         | Connection rejected or authenticated                | ŌØī Not implemented       |
| QS-04 | Maintainability       | Developer adds a new form field; change propagates to all layers            | Single-point change in `ModelProperties` enum       | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Partial (MVP only)   |
| QS-05 | Testability           | Developer unit-tests `searchPerson()` without a browser                     | Isolated Jest test passes                           | ŌØī No tests exist        |
| QS-06 | Usability             | Caseworker searches by partial name; results appear immediately              | In-memory filter, instant response                  | Ō£ģ Met                   |
| QS-07 | Correctness           | JSON message with unknown `target` arrives at Swing client                  | Silently ignored ŌĆö falls through switch statement   | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Falls through, no log |
| QS-08 | Portability           | System runs on Windows 10/11 and Linux                                      | All runtimes are cross-platform                     | Ō£ģ Met                   |
| QS-09 | Security              | IBAN/personal data transmitted over WebSocket                               | Encrypted with TLS (WSS)                            | ŌØī Not encrypted         |
| QS-10 | Scalability           | 10 concurrent caseworkers use the search UI simultaneously                  | Isolated sessions; no data cross-contamination       | ŌØī Broadcast leaks data  |

### 10.3 Code Metrics Summary

| Component               | Lines of Code (approx.) | Test Coverage | Key Complexity Issues                                     |
|-------------------------|------------------------|---------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| `WebsocketServer.js`    | ~68                    | 0%            | Simple callbacks; complexity in broadcast loop            |
| `Search.vue`            | ~250                   | 0%            | High cyclomatic in `searchPerson()`; hardcoded data       |
| `websocket/Main.java`   | ~458                   | 0%            | Very high ŌĆö monolithic; manual streaming JSON parse       |
| `PocView.java`          | ~203                   | 0%            | Low ŌĆö layout-only; no business logic                      |
| `PocPresenter.java`     | ~113                   | 0%            | Medium ŌĆö `DocumentListener` boilerplate for each field    |
| `PocModel.java`         | ~49                    | 0%            | Low-medium ŌĆö clean delegation pattern                     |
| `HttpBinService.java`   | ~38                    | 0%            | Low ŌĆö straightforward HTTP POST with no error handling    |

---

## 11. Risks and Technical Debt

### 11.1 Risk Matrix

```mermaid
quadrantChart
    title Risk Matrix ŌĆö Likelihood vs. Impact
    x-axis Low Likelihood --> High Likelihood
    y-axis Low Impact --> High Impact
    quadrant-1 Critical ŌĆö Act Now
    quadrant-2 Monitor
    quadrant-3 Accept
    quadrant-4 Mitigate
    No Authentication: [0.85, 0.90]
    No TLS/WSS: [0.80, 0.85]
    Zero Test Coverage: [0.95, 0.70]
    No Error Handling: [0.70, 0.65]
    Broadcast Data Leak: [0.70, 0.60]
    No Reconnect Logic: [0.75, 0.55]
    Hardcoded localhost URLs: [0.90, 0.50]
    Monolithic WS Main: [0.60, 0.45]
    Hardcoded Test Data: [0.95, 0.30]
    CORS Not Enforced: [0.80, 0.60]
```

### 11.2 Risk Register

| ID    | Risk                               | Likelihood | Impact | Description                                                                                  | Mitigation Strategy                                                         |
|-------|------------------------------------|------------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| R-01  | **No Authentication**              | High       | High   | Any client on the local network can connect to port 1337 and receive/inject all broadcast data | Implement token-based auth (JWT in WS handshake header) before production   |
| R-02  | **No Transport Encryption (TLS)**  | High       | High   | IBAN, DOB, personal addresses transmitted in plaintext; GDPR-relevant PII at risk            | Switch to `wss://` with a valid TLS certificate; enforce HTTPS on Vue server |
| R-03  | **Zero Automated Tests**           | Certain    | High   | No unit, integration, or E2E tests across any component; regressions are invisible           | Add Jest (Vue), JUnit 5 (Java), Mocha/Jest (Node.js)                        |
| R-04  | **No Error Handling**              | High       | Medium | WS server crash, HTTP 4xx/5xx, or malformed JSON causes unhandled exceptions across all layers | Add `try/catch`, WS `onerror` handlers, HTTP status validation              |
| R-05  | **Broadcast Data Leakage**         | High       | Medium | All clients receive all messages; multi-user deployment leaks data between sessions           | Implement session/room-based routing in the WS server                       |
| R-06  | **Hardcoded localhost URLs**       | Certain    | Medium | `ws://localhost:1337` and `http://localhost:8080` prevent deployment outside a local machine  | Externalize via `.env` (Vue), `application.properties`, or env variables    |
| R-07  | **No WebSocket Reconnection**      | High       | Medium | Server restart leaves all clients disconnected with no recovery; application appears frozen   | Implement exponential backoff reconnection in Vue and Swing clients          |
| R-08  | **CORS Not Validated**             | High       | Medium | `request.accept(null, request.origin)` accepts connections from any origin                   | Validate `request.origin` against an allowlist of permitted origins         |
| R-09  | **Monolithic `websocket/Main.java`** | Medium   | Low    | All concerns (UI, WS, JSON) in one class makes testing and maintenance difficult              | Refactor using MVP pattern from the `com.poc` variant                       |
| R-10  | **Hardcoded Test Data in Vue**     | Certain    | Low    | Search data in `Search.vue` requires code change and rebuild for any data update              | Replace with REST API call using `api.yml` contract                         |

### 11.3 Technical Debt Backlog

| ID    | Debt Item                                           | Effort  | Priority | Recommendation                                                    |
|-------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| TD-01 | Add WebSocket reconnection to Vue.js client          | Low     | High     | Add `socket.onclose` with exponential backoff timer               |
| TD-02 | Add WebSocket reconnection to Swing client           | Low     | High     | Implement reconnect in `onClose` with `CountDownLatch` reset      |
| TD-03 | Add test suite for Vue.js `searchPerson()` logic     | Low     | High     | Pure function ŌĆö trivially testable with Jest                      |
| TD-04 | Add unit tests for `PocModel.action()`               | Low     | High     | Inject `HttpBinService` mock; verify state transitions            |
| TD-05 | Externalize configuration (all hardcoded URLs)       | Low     | High     | `.env` for Vue, env vars or properties file for Java              |
| TD-06 | Replace streaming JSON parser in `websocket.Main`   | Medium  | Medium   | Use Jackson or Gson for simpler, less error-prone deserialization |
| TD-07 | Merge duplicate Swing UI layout code                 | Medium  | Medium   | Both variants have identical `GridBagLayout` ŌĆö extract to shared `PocView` |
| TD-08 | Add WS server error handling and structured logging  | Low     | Medium   | Add `connection.on('error')` handler; replace `console.log` with a logger |
| TD-09 | Implement HTTP status validation in `HttpBinService` | Low     | Medium   | Check `responseCode` before reading body; throw typed exceptions  |
| TD-10 | Add HTTPS/WSS support                               | Medium  | High     | Required for any non-local environment; PII data requires encryption |
| TD-11 | Replace hardcoded person data with REST API call    | High    | Low (PoC)| Implement `GET /search?q=...` endpoint backed by real data store  |

---

## 12. Glossary

| Term                              | Definition                                                                                         |
|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **ALLEGRO**                       | Legacy Java Swing desktop application used by caseworkers for customer and insurance data management |
| **Anordnen**                      | German: "Arrange/Order" ŌĆö button label in Swing UI triggering HTTP form submission                  |
| **BG-Nummer**                     | Berufsgenossenschaftsnummer ŌĆö employer's liability insurance association number                     |
| **BIC**                           | Bank Identifier Code ŌĆö standardised code identifying a financial institution                        |
| **Betriebsbezeichnung**           | German: Company / employer designation                                                              |
| **CountDownLatch**                | Java concurrency primitive used in both Swing variants to keep the main thread alive until WS close |
| **DocumentListener**              | Java Swing event interface; `PocPresenter` uses it to detect field text changes in real time        |
| **EventEmitter**                  | Observer pattern component in `com.poc.model`; maintains a list of `EventListener` subscribers     |
| **EventListener**                 | Interface in `com.poc.model`; implementors receive string events via `onEvent(String)`              |
| **Geburtsdatum**                  | German: Date of birth                                                                               |
| **Geschlecht**                    | German: Gender (Weiblich = Female, M├żnnlich = Male, Divers = Diverse)                              |
| **IBAN**                          | International Bank Account Number ŌĆö standardised identifier for bank accounts                       |
| **javax.websocket (JSR-356)**     | Java EE standard API for WebSocket clients and servers; implemented by Tyrus                        |
| **Kunde / Kunden**                | German: Customer(s) ŌĆö the person records managed in the system                                     |
| **Kundennummer (knr)**            | German: Customer number ŌĆö unique identifier for a Kunde                                             |
| **Leistung**                      | German: Benefit or service entitlement in a social insurance context                                |
| **ModelProperties**               | Java enum in `com.poc.model` defining all form field keys; drives the `EnumMap` in `PocModel`      |
| **MVP**                           | Model-View-Presenter ŌĆö UI pattern separating visual components (View) from business logic (Model) with a coordinating Presenter |
| **Nach ALLEGRO ├╝bernehmen**       | German: "Transfer to ALLEGRO" ŌĆö the key action button in the Vue.js UI                             |
| **Node.js WS Server**             | The central Node.js WebSocket hub at `node-server/src/WebsocketServer.js`                           |
| **Ort**                           | German: City / town                                                                                 |
| **PLZ**                           | Postleitzahl ŌĆö German postal code (ZIP code)                                                        |
| **PoC**                           | Proof of Concept ŌĆö a prototype demonstrating technical and architectural feasibility                 |
| **PocModel**                      | The M in MVP; holds all form field state as `EnumMap<ModelProperties, ValueModel<?>>`              |
| **PocPresenter**                  | The P in MVP; wires PocView events to PocModel and triggers the `action()` workflow                 |
| **PocView**                       | The V in MVP; owns and lays out all Java Swing UI widgets                                           |
| **RFC 6455**                      | IETF standard defining the WebSocket protocol                                                       |
| **RV-Nummer**                     | Rentenversicherungsnummer ŌĆö German pension insurance number                                         |
| **Sachbearbeiter**                | German: Caseworker ŌĆö the primary human user of both client applications                             |
| **Search Mock**                   | The title of the Vue.js application ŌĆö a simulated search interface                                  |
| **Strasse**                       | German: Street                                                                                      |
| **Suchen**                        | German: "Search" ŌĆö button label in the Vue.js search form                                           |
| **Tr├żger-Nr. der gE.**            | Tr├żger-Nummer der gemeinsamen Einrichtung ŌĆö joint employment agency carrier number                  |
| **Tyrus**                         | GlassFish Tyrus ŌĆö the reference implementation of JSR-356 (javax.websocket) for Java               |
| **ValueModel\<T\>**               | Generic wrapper class in `com.poc`; holds a single typed value with get/set accessor                |
| **Vorname**                       | German: First name                                                                                  |
| **Vorsatzwort**                   | German name particle (e.g., "von", "van") ŌĆö nobility/particle prefix in a surname                  |
| **Vue.js**                        | Progressive JavaScript framework (v2.x) used for the browser-based search client                   |
| **WebSocket**                     | Full-duplex, persistent communication protocol over a single TCP connection (RFC 6455)              |
| **Zahlungsempf├żnger**             | German: Payment recipient ŌĆö entity with IBAN/BIC receiving payments on behalf of a Kunde           |

---

*End of Arc42 Architecture Documentation ŌĆö Allegro PoC*

> **Document Generation Notes:**  
> This documentation was generated by automated source code analysis of the following files:
> - `node-server/src/WebsocketServer.js` ŌĆö Node.js WebSocket hub server  
> - `node-vue-client/src/App.vue`, `Search.vue`, `main.js` ŌĆö Vue.js SPA  
> - `swing/src/main/java/websocket/Main.java` ŌĆö Java Swing WebSocket client  
> - `swing/src/main/java/com/Main.java` ŌĆö MVP variant entry point  
> - `swing/src/main/java/com/poc/model/*` ŌĆö MVP model layer  
> - `swing/src/main/java/com/poc/presentation/*` ŌĆö MVP presentation layer  
> - `pom.xml` ŌĆö Maven build configuration (Java 22, Tyrus 1.15, javax.json)  
> - `api.yml` ŌĆö OpenAPI 3.0.1 specification  
> - `README.md`, `node-vue-client/README.md`, `node-server/doc/Readme.txt` ŌĆö Project setup documentation
